prompt:
We announce maintenance windows for the fleet by hand-typing a card into the ops channel, and every third one has a typo'd time or a made-up impact level. I want a small PowerShell tool, `announce.ps1`, in the repo root, that formats the card and refuses garbage. Our CI and the chat-bridge both run it non-interactively (`pwsh -NoProfile -NonInteractive -File announce.ps1 ...`), so it must NEVER sit waiting for input — if a required parameter is missing, PowerShell's own binding error on stderr is exactly what we want.

Do it with a real `param()` block, not hand-rolled `$args` parsing — that's the whole point. Typed and attribute-validated:

- `-Service <name>` (mandatory): must match `^[a-z][a-z0-9-]{1,31}$`, enforced with `[ValidatePattern()]` so a bad id dies at binding time.
- `-Date <yyyy-MM-dd>` (mandatory): shape `^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$`, also `[ValidatePattern()]`.
- `-Impact <level>`: `[ValidateSet()]` over low, normal, urgent; default normal.
- `-Minutes <n>`: a real `[int]` (so `-Minutes forty` is a binding failure), default 30. The 1..480 range check however is OURS, in the body, because operators need the usage reminder with it: out of range prints `announce: minutes out of range: <n>` then the usage block, both to stderr, exit 64.
- `-Start <HH:mm>`: default `22:00`. Validated in the body (same reason): 24-hour, two digits each side, so `9:30` and `24:00` are both `announce: invalid start time: <v>` + usage to stderr, exit 64.
- `-NotesFile <path>`: optional. Each line of the file becomes a `note: <line>` card line, trailing whitespace trimmed, blank lines dropped. File missing: `announce: notes file not found: <path>` on stderr, exit 65, no usage block (data errors don't get one). File with no usable lines: `announce: notes file is empty: <path>`, exit 65.
- `-Help`: prints the usage block to stdout and exits 0 — and it has to work on its own, WITHOUT the mandatory parameters getting in the way (hint: that's what parameter sets are for; I don't care how you do it as long as `-Help` alone is clean).

The card on stdout, exactly this shape (LF line endings):

service: api-gw
window: 2026-08-15 07:45-09:15
impact: low
minutes: 90

The window line is `<date> <start>-<end>`, end computed from start + minutes. If the window crosses midnight, tag the end time with `+1d` (e.g. `23:50-00:35+1d`; ending exactly at midnight is `00:00+1d`). Note lines, if any, come after `minutes:`.

Exit contract: 0 ok; 64 usage errors we detect ourselves (message + usage block on stderr); 65 data errors (message only on stderr); and anything pwsh's parameter binder refuses (missing mandatory, ValidateSet/ValidatePattern violations, bad int, stray positional args) exits 1 with the engine's error — leave those to the engine, do NOT pre-chew them.

The usage block, byte-exact (the tests compare it verbatim):

usage: announce.ps1 -Service <name> -Date <yyyy-MM-dd> [options]

options:
  -Service <name>     lowercase service id (letters, digits, dashes)
  -Date <yyyy-MM-dd>  date of the window
  -Start <HH:mm>      24-hour start time (default 22:00)
  -Minutes <n>        window length in minutes, 1..480 (default 30)
  -Impact <level>     one of: low, normal, urgent (default normal)
  -NotesFile <path>   append each non-empty line of this file to the card
  -Help               print this help and exit 0

exit codes: 0 ok, 64 usage error, 65 data error (binding errors exit 1)

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_announce.ps1` from the workspace root must pass unmodified — don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P1 item 1: [CmdletBinding()]/param() CLI ergonomics. Pins probed 2026-07-16 on pwsh 7.6.3: mandatory-miss under -NoProfile -NonInteractive -File exits 1 with 'Cannot process command because of one or more missing mandatory parameters' on stderr and EMPTY stdout (no prompt); ValidateSet/ValidatePattern/int-transformation/positional-refusal all exit 1 with stable message fragments; -Help works despite mandatory params via a separate parameter set (probed with DefaultParameterSetName='Run'). Script-owned checks (start time, minutes range) yield 64+usage vs attribute checks yielding engine exit 1 — that split IS the lesson (attribute metadata + typed binding, not getopts; differentiates from bash-subcmd whose pins are getopts mechanics). No existing seed covers PowerShell param attributes; domain (maintenance-window card formatter) overlaps no existing seed.
